title: 2017 Hull Kingston Rovers season

During the season, they competed in the [[2017 RFL Championship]], securing immediate promotion back to the [[Super League]], and the [[2017 Challenge Cup]]. Following the sacking of head coach [[Chris Chester (rugby league)|Chris Chester]] during the [[2016 Hull Kingston Rovers season|2016 season]], Hull Kingston Rovers announced in September 2016 that former [[Australia national rugby league team]] and [[Wests Tigers]] coach [[Tim Sheens]] would be the club's head coach from the 2017 season onwards.{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=Hull KR confirm appointment of Tim Sheens as new coach |url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-kr-confirm-appointment-tim-sheens-new-coach/ |access-date=5 November 2024 |work=Total Rugby League |date=9 September 2016}} ==Preseason friendlies== {{table alignment}} {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and time!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!Report |- | 8 January; 15:00 | {{leagueicon|Hull FC|16}} [[Hull F.C.]] | A | [[KCOM Stadium]] | {{WinDL|res=W}} | 40–16 | [[Adam Quinlan|Quinlan]], [[Jamie Ellis|Ellis]], [[Danny Addy|Addy]], [[David Hodgson (rugby league)|Hodgson]], [[Andrew Heffernan (rugby league)|Heffernan]], [[Will Oakes|Oakes]], Milton | (7/7) | Unknown |{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=REPORT: Derby joy for Hull KR in Tim Sheens' first game |url=https://www.totalrl.com/report-derby-joy-hull-kr-tim-sheens-first-game/ |access-date=5 November 2024 |work=Total Rugby League |date=8 January 2017}} |- | 20 January, 14:30 | {{leagueicon|Leeds|16}} [[Leeds Rhinos]] | A | [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]] | {{WinDL|res=L}} | 4–30 | | (0/1) | Unknown |{{cite news |last1=Westmorland |first1=Gareth |title=Hull KR 2017 season review -
